title: acaena craspedotricha
intro: Acaena craspedotricha, also known as the variegated woolly-pod or woolly-head, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits associated with Acaena craspedotricha include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to ease muscle spasms and relieve digestive issues. Acaena craspedotricha can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and its leaves and stems can be used in traditional medicine, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
